description: The hard-sell contractor: a $7500 headline bid (median $12000, floor $8400) that isn't binding AND demands a 40% deposit. All three signals - below-market, non-binding, oversized deposit - must fire.

job_fields.scope_summary: gut and redo a 5x8 hall bathroom

simulated_conversation.message: Bid on a 5x8 bathroom remodel, full gut, standard finishes.
Premier Renovations! Oh we can absolutely do that - seventy five hundred, that's our special right now. Cheapest you'll find, guaranteed.
Seventy five hundred all in? Does that include demo, materials, and the permit for the plumbing?
That's the starting point! We'll dial in the exact fixtures once we get going - you'll want the upgraded tile trust me. To lock this price in I just need a forty percent deposit today.
So the seventy five hundred isn't a firm final number, and you need three thousand up front?
Forty percent locks your spot and the price. Everyone does it this way.

tool_calls.params_as_json: {"outcome": "quote_given", "company_name": "Premier Renovations", "base_price": 7500, "line_items": [{"label": "Headline remodel price", "amount": 7500, "is_optional_or_conditional": false}, {"label": "Required deposit", "amount": 3000, "is_optional_or_conditional": false}], "total_price": 7500, "binding": false, "notes": "Would not commit to a firm number; wants 40% deposit up front."}
